audit_source.population_density_sq_mi: Derived from population and area estimate
audit_source.rent_burden_pct: Estimated based on PUMS data for Travis County

demographics.audit_notes: Data for 1990 reflects the area before major development. Values are based on 1990 Census data for the relevant block groups that would become Tract 24.26. Some new fields are estimated due to lack of direct sources for this period.
All values are interpolated between the 1990 and 2000 decennial censuses. They appear directionally correct for the period.

audit_source.rent_burden_pct: Estimated based on PUMS data for Travis County

demographics.audit_notes: Values align with 2000 Census data for Tract 24.26. The area is beginning to show signs of increased density and diversity.
Values are interpolated. This period marks the beginning of major redevelopment and residential construction, leading to rapid population growth.

Massive population spike (89% since 2005) is confirmed by housing unit data, reflecting the construction of thousands of new apartment units. Data is an aggregate of multiple tracts to represent the neighborhood.
Continued rapid population growth (73% since 2010) is consistent with ongoing large-scale apartment development in the area.
Population growth continues, though the rate has started to slow compared to the previous decade. Values align with 2020 Census and ACS data.
Values are consistent with the latest available ACS 5-year estimates.
Values are consistent with the latest available ACS 5-year estimates.
Values are extrapolated based on trends from the most recent ACS data. Considered plausible projections.
Values are projections based on recent trends. Confidence is medium as they represent future estimates.
Values are projections based on recent trends. Confidence is medium as they represent future estimates.

audit_source.median_property_tax: Calculated from median value and historical tax rates
audit_source.new_construction_permits: City of Austin historical permit data estimate

property.audit_notes: Property data aligns with 1990 Census for the area. Homeownership was high before the wave of apartment construction.
Values are interpolated and appear plausible for the mid-1990s Austin market.

audit_source.new_construction_permits: City of Austin permit data estimate

property.audit_notes: Housing unit growth is accelerating. Home values reflect the dot-com era boom in Austin.

audit_source.new_construction_permits: City of Austin permit data estimate

property.audit_notes: This period marks the beginning of massive apartment construction. The sharp drop in homeownership rate and spike in housing units is plausible.
The explosion in housing units (83% increase since 2005) is confirmed by Census data and reflects the build-out of the area with large apartment complexes.
Continued strong growth in housing units and home values. The year-over-year value change reflects the rapid market appreciation post-Great Recession.
Home value spike is dramatic but consistent with Austin's market from 2015-2020. Housing construction continues at a strong pace.
The 20% jump in home value reflects the intense post-pandemic housing market in Austin.
Values align with market data for the peak of the recent housing boom.
CORRECTION: Original median_home_value of $525,000 was too high. Corrected to $485,000 to reflect the market cooling in late 2022 and 2023. This results in a negative Y-o-Y change.
CORRECTION: Original median_home_value of $540,000 was too optimistic. Corrected to $495,000 for a modest recovery. Projections for current/future years carry lower confidence.
CORRECTION: Original median_home_value of $560,000 was too optimistic. Corrected to $510,000 for a modest recovery. All values are projections with low confidence.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Commercial sq ft reflects the original, fully operational Southpark Meadows Mall. Newly added fields are null or estimated due to lack of data for this period.
Values are interpolated and plausible for the period.

CORRECTION: Original commercial_development_sq_ft of 800,000 was high for the mall's 'dead mall' status. Corrected to 450,000 to reflect high vacancy before demolition and redevelopment. All other values align with Census 2000.
Commercial sq ft begins to increase as the new open-air shopping center development begins construction.
The massive spike in commercial sq ft is accurate, reflecting the new 1.6 million sq ft Southpark Meadows shopping center. Higher unemployment reflects the Great Recession.
Data aligns with ACS estimates for the period. Economic indicators show recovery from the recession.
Unemployment rate reflects initial COVID-19 pandemic impact. Eviction rate is lower due to pandemic-era moratoriums.
Values align with the latest available ACS data.
Eviction filing rate increases as pandemic-era protections expire. Other values align with latest ACS data.
Values are extrapolated from recent trends and appear plausible.
All values are projections with low confidence.
All values are projections with low confidence.
